properties.displayName: Suspicious Email Interaction with Downstream Execution IOC
properties.description:
This rule detects inbound emails that have failed SPF and/or DKIM authentication checks and presents evidence of post-delivery user interaction. Authentication failures on inbound email are a strong indicator of email spoofing or unauthorized sending infrastructure, commonly associated with phishing campaigns targeting organizational users.
Unlike basic phishing detections that alert on email receipt alone, this rule correlates authentication failures with downstream activity to identify attacks that have progressed beyond the initial delivery phase of the MITRE ATT&CK kill chain.
properties.query:
// Identify inbound emails that failed SPF or DKIM authentication checks, indicating potential spoofing or unauthorized sending infrastructure
let failAuthEmails = EmailEvents
    | extend 
        AuthenticationDetails = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
    | where 
        EmailDirection == "Inbound" and 
        (
            AuthenticationDetails.SPF has_any ("none", "fail") or 
            AuthenticationDetails.DKIM has_any ("none", "fail", "error")
            )
    | summarize by 
        NetworkMessageId
    ;
// Retrieve URL click events associated with authentication-failed emails to detect potential phishing link engagement
let badClicks = UrlClickEvents
    | where 
        NetworkMessageId has_any (failAuthEmails)
    | summarize by
        NetworkMessageId, Url
    ;
// Extract SHA-256 file hash indicators from the Threat Intelligence table for use in malicious attachment correlation
let tiHash = ThreatIntelIndicators
    | where 
        ObservableKey == "file:hashes.'SHA-256'"
    | summarize by
        ObservableValue
;
// Correlate email attachments from authentication-failed emails against known malicious SHA-256 hashes in the Threat Intelligence table
let badAttach = EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where 
        NetworkMessageId has_any (failAuthEmails)
    | summarize by 
        NetworkMessageId, SHA256
    | join 
        kind=inner tiHash on $left.SHA256 == $right.ObservableValue
    | project-rename 
        attachNetworkMessageId=NetworkMessageId, attachSHA256=SHA256
;
// Detect files created on endpoints where the SHA-256 hash matches an attachment delivered via an authentication-failed email, indicating a potential payload execution
let badFilEvent = DeviceFileEvents
    | where 
        ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | join 
        kind=inner (
            EmailAttachmentInfo
                | where
                    NetworkMessageId has_any (failAuthEmails)
                | summarize by 
                    NetworkMessageId, SHA256
                )
            on $left.SHA256 == $right.SHA256
    | project-rename 
        fileNetworkMessageId=NetworkMessageId, fileSHA256=SHA256
;
// Consolidate all identified indicators of compromise (IOCs) — malicious URLs, attachment hashes, and file event hashes — into a unified dataset per email
let badEvents = badClicks
    | join 
        kind=fullouter badAttach on $left.NetworkMessageId == $right.attachNetworkMessageId
    | join 
        kind=fullouter badFilEvent on $left.NetworkMessageId == $right.fileNetworkMessageId
    | extend 
        IOC = pack_array(Url, attachSHA256, fileSHA256)
    | mv-expand 
        IOC to typeof(string)
    | where 
        isnotempty(IOC)
    | project 
        NetworkMessageId, IOC
;
// Return full email metadata for messages with confirmed IOCs, enriched with authentication results and associated indicators for analyst review
EmailEvents
    | join
        kind=inner badEvents on $left.NetworkMessageId == $right.NetworkMessageId 
    | extend 
        AuthenticationDetails = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
    | project 
        TimeGenerated, 
        Subject, 
        SenderFromAddress, 
        RecipientEmailAddress, 
        AuthenticationDetails.SPF, 
        AuthenticationDetails.DKIM,
        AuthenticationDetails.DMARC,
        InternetMessageId,
        NetworkMessageId,
        IOC
